description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a symmetrical facade and a steeply pitched hip roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The front features two prominent bay windows on either side of a central double-door entryway, each with its own small overhang. The roof is accented by two brick chimneys and gray trim along the eaves. The rear of the house is simpler, with two single doors and a pair of vertically aligned windows. The side elevations are plain, showing the expanse of brickwork and the roof's overhang. The overall design suggests a traditional British or Edwardian semi-detached style, suitable for urban or suburban neighborhoods.
description: A two-story brick house with a red-tiled hip roof, bay windows, and a symmetrical front entryway. The design is traditional and urban, with prominent chimneys and classic architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing two bay windows, a central double-door entry, and the main roof with chimneys.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the plain brick wall, roof overhang, and both chimneys visible on the roof ridge.
description_view_2: Rear view with two single doors, two vertically aligned windows, and a simpler facade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the plain brick wall and roof overhang.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with bay windows on the front
attribute_entryway_design: Central double-door entry with small overhangs, flanked by bay windows and accessed by a short step
